headline: Morocco hold Brazil 1-1 — the Atlas Lions roar again, Brazil's drought wobbles in game one
summary: In their WC2026 Group C opener (13 Jun, MetLife), 2022 semifinalists Morocco held five-time champions Brazil to a 1-1 draw. Ismael Saibari chipped Alisson to put the Atlas Lions ahead (21'); Vinicius Junior rescued a point for Carlo Ancelotti's Brazil (32'). Keeper Yassine Bounou stood firm through ~10 minutes of stoppage time as Brazil pressed. Africa's standard-bearers proved 2022 was no fluke; Brazil — chasing a first World Cup since 2002 under their first foreign coach — dropped points in game one.
angles: Morocco 2022 was no fluke — they just held Brazil. Is Morocco the most dangerous dark horse in 2026?
Brazil dropped points in game one under Ancelotti — is the 24-year drought in their heads?
Vinicius saved them, but who is Ismael Saibari — the man who chipped Alisson?
Bounou vs Brazil: the keeper who refused to break
